title: [policy] OSCORE end-to-end encryption compliance
description: Policy: spec/06-security.md §8.7. Application payloads MUST be OSCORE encrypted for E2E security. Link signatures provide hop-by-hop auth but not confidentiality. When executing: grep for CoAP/application payload handling, verify OSCORE encryption, create compliance bead per code section.
close_reason:
VIOLATIONS FOUND: Application payloads are NOT OSCORE encrypted by default, violating spec/06-security.md section 8.7.

Audit findings:
1. Python: OSCORE library exists (lichen.crypto.oscore) but NOT integrated into CoAP transport (transport.py, node_channel.py send plaintext)
2. C firmware: FIXME(OSCORE) comments indicate OSCORE not yet implemented
3. Rust: SecureStack exists but Stack (plaintext) is used by default in tests/examples

Filed 3 sub-beads for remediation:
- project-LICHEN-r6fu.1: Python CoAP OSCORE integration
- project-LICHEN-r6fu.2: C firmware OSCORE implementation
- project-LICHEN-r6fu.3: Rust SecureStack as default

OSCORE building blocks exist but E2E encryption is opt-in rather than enforced. Policy requires OSCORE for all CoAP traffic.
